model.cfpb_complaints.dim_date.name: dim_date
model.cfpb_complaints.dim_date.description: Date spine from 2011-01-01 to 2023-12-31, one row per calendar day. Generated via BigQuery GENERATE_DATE_ARRAY — no dbt-utils dependency.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.description: Calendar date — primary key and FK target for fact tables.

model.cfpb_complaints.dim_date.raw_code:
with date_spine as (
    select date_val
    from unnest(generate_date_array('2011-01-01', '2023-12-31', interval 1 day)) as date_val
)

select
    date_val                              as date_day,
    extract(year from date_val)           as year,
    extract(quarter from date_val)        as quarter,
    extract(month from date_val)          as month_num,
    format_date('%B', date_val)           as month_name,
    extract(week from date_val)           as week_of_year,
    extract(dayofweek from date_val)      as day_of_week,
    format_date('%A', date_val)           as day_name,
    date_trunc(date_val, month)           as first_day_of_month,
    date_trunc(date_val, quarter)         as first_day_of_quarter,
    date_trunc(date_val, year)            as first_day_of_year
from date_spine

snapshot.cfpb_complaints.company_renames.name: company_renames
snapshot.cfpb_complaints.company_renames.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        target_schema='snapshots',
        unique_key='raw_company_name',
        strategy='check',
        check_cols=['canonical_name', 'fdic_top_holder'],
    )
}}

-- Tracks canonical company name and FDIC parent changes over time.
--
-- In production on a live complaint feed, dbt would detect when canonical_name
-- or fdic_top_holder changes for a given raw_company_name and write a new row
-- with dbt_valid_from = change timestamp, closing the prior row's dbt_valid_to.
-- The result would automatically capture rebrands (SunTrust → Truist, etc.)
-- as they appear in the source data.
--
-- With this frozen source (last updated 2023-03-25), the snapshot establishes
-- initial state only — no future changes will be observed. The three known
-- rebrands are instead encoded via the crosswalk's parent_as_of column:
--   SunTrust → Truist:              2019-12-01
--   BB&T → Truist:                  2019-12-01
--   Alliance Data → Bread Financial: 2021-02-01
-- Query stg_company_crosswalk WHERE parent_as_of IS NOT NULL to surface them.
--
-- Requires stg_company_crosswalk to be built first (uv run dbt run -s stg_company_crosswalk).

select
    raw_company_name,
    canonical_name,
    category,
    fdic_top_holder,
    parent_as_of
from {{ ref('stg_company_crosswalk') }}

model.cfpb_complaints.stg_company_crossw.name: stg_company_crosswalk
model.cfpb_complaints.stg_company_crossw.description: Typed staging pass over the company_crosswalk seed. raw_company_name is normalized identically to stg_cfpb_complaints.company_name_normalized (UPPER + collapsed whitespace + trailing punct stripped) so the join in int_complaints_with_company works on equal keys. One row per raw CFPB company name variant.

raw_company_name.name: raw_company_name
raw_company_name.description: Normalized CFPB company name variant — join key to company_name_normalized in stg_cfpb_complaints. Same normalization expression applied to both sides.

canonical_name.name: canonical_name
canonical_name.description: Human-readable company name used as the grouping key in dim_company (e.g. "JPMorgan Chase", "Truist"). Multiple raw variants map to one canonical name; rebranded entities (SunTrust, BB&T) also map to Truist.

category.name: category
category.description: Company type. Controls which downstream enrichments apply: only bank-category rows join to FDIC data.

fdic_top_holder.name: fdic_top_holder
fdic_top_holder.description: Raw FDIC top_holder value used to join to int_fdic_banks_normalized. Null for non-bank categories and for bank entities with no FDIC record (e.g. foreign-parented subsidiaries). Pre-merger SunTrust and BB&T entries carry historically-correct values (SUNTRUST BANKS INC, BB&T CORP) but these no longer exist in the current FDIC active-institutions snapshot.

parent_as_of.name: parent_as_of
parent_as_of.description: Effective date of entity change for known rebrands. Null means the row represents a current (or historically stable) entity. Non-null rows: SunTrust → Truist (2019-12-01), BB&T → Truist (2019-12-01), Alliance Data → Bread Financial (2021-02-01). Used by the company_renames snapshot to surface SCD2 rebrand history.

model.cfpb_complaints.stg_company_crossw.raw_code:
-- Thin staging pass over the company_crosswalk seed.
-- raw_company_name is normalized identically to stg_cfpb_complaints.company_name_normalized
-- so the join in int_complaints_with_company works on equal keys.

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('company_crosswalk') }}
),

staged as (
    select
        regexp_replace(
            regexp_replace(upper(trim(raw_company_name)), r'\s+', ' '),
            r'[.,;]+$', ''
        )                               as raw_company_name,

        trim(canonical_name)            as canonical_name,
        trim(category)                  as category,
        trim(fdic_top_holder)           as fdic_top_holder,
        cast(parent_as_of as date)      as parent_as_of
    from source
)

select * from staged

model.cfpb_complaints.dim_company.name: dim_company
model.cfpb_complaints.dim_company.description: One row per canonical company in the crosswalk (~30 entities after deduplication). Grain is canonical_name — multiple raw CFPB name variants and pre-merger rebrand rows collapse to a single dim row. Temporal stats (first/last seen, volume) are computed from fct_complaints and reflect all complaint eras for that canonical entity.

canonical_name.name: canonical_name
canonical_name.description: Human-readable company name from the crosswalk seed.

category.name: category
category.description: Company type: bank, credit_bureau, credit_union, debt_collector, mortgage_servicer, student_loan_servicer, fintech, or other.

is_credit_bureau.name: is_credit_bureau
is_credit_bureau.description: TRUE for Equifax, TransUnion, Experian, LexisNexis.

fdic_top_holder.name: fdic_top_holder
fdic_top_holder.description: FDIC top_holder key for the current entity. Populated for bank rows only. Pre-merger rebrand rows (SunTrust, BB&T) carry historically-correct top_holders that no longer appear in the active FDIC snapshot — see DECISIONS.md.

first_seen_date.name: first_seen_date
first_seen_date.description: Earliest date_received across all complaints for this canonical company.

last_seen_date.name: last_seen_date
last_seen_date.description: Latest date_received across all complaints for this canonical company.

total_complaint_volume.name: total_complaint_volume
total_complaint_volume.description: Total complaint count across all crosswalked raw name variants.

model.cfpb_complaints.dim_company.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

with crosswalk as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_company_crosswalk') }}
),

-- Deduplicate to one row per canonical company.
-- Truist has 3 crosswalk rows (SunTrust, BB&T, post-merger Truist); the current
-- fdic_top_holder is the one where parent_as_of IS NULL.
companies as (
    select
        canonical_name,
        category,
        max(case when parent_as_of is null then fdic_top_holder end) as fdic_top_holder
    from crosswalk
    group by canonical_name, category
),

complaint_stats as (
    select
        canonical_name,
        min(date_received)  as first_seen_date,
        max(date_received)  as last_seen_date,
        count(*)            as total_complaint_volume
    from {{ ref('fct_complaints') }}
    where is_crosswalked
    group by canonical_name
)

select
    farm_fingerprint(c.canonical_name)  as company_sk,
    c.canonical_name,
    c.category,
    c.category = 'credit_bureau'        as is_credit_bureau,
    c.fdic_top_holder,
    s.first_seen_date,
    s.last_seen_date,
    s.total_complaint_volume
from companies c
left join complaint_stats s
    on c.canonical_name = s.canonical_name

model.cfpb_complaints.fct_complaints.name: fct_complaints
model.cfpb_complaints.fct_complaints.description: One row per CFPB complaint (2012–2022, ~3.4M rows). Product and issue columns carried directly — no dim join required. company_name_normalized is kept as a debug column alongside canonical_name and category from the crosswalk. company_sk enrichment deferred to Phase 2 (dim_company).

subproduct.name: subproduct
subproduct.description: Raw subproduct label from CFPB. Nullable.

product_normalized.name: product_normalized
product_normalized.description: Legacy CFPB product labels mapped to current taxonomy via product_mapping seed. Consumer Loan split into Vehicle loan or lease vs. Payday loan, title loan, or personal loan based on subproduct.

product_category.name: product_category
product_category.description: Seven-bucket grouping of product_normalized: mortgage, credit_reporting, debt_collection, banking (checking/savings), card (credit card + prepaid), payments (money transfer/virtual currency), other (student loans, auto, payday, misc). Card is split from banking — complaint patterns and volumes differ significantly. Deliberate denormalization — dim_product was dropped since this single attribute doesn't justify a separate dim. See DECISIONS.md.

subproduct_normalized.name: subproduct_normalized
subproduct_normalized.description: Legacy subproduct labels mapped to current taxonomy via subproduct_mapping seed. Null-subproduct cases for legacy single-category products filled with best-fit current label.

company_sk.name: company_sk
company_sk.description: FK to dim_company. FARM_FINGERPRINT(canonical_name). NULL for the ~26% of complaints not matched by the crosswalk.

company_name_normalized.name: company_name_normalized
company_name_normalized.description: Uppercased, whitespace-collapsed, trailing-punct-stripped form of company_name. Kept as a debug column alongside canonical_name.

canonical_name.name: canonical_name
canonical_name.description: Human-readable company name from the crosswalk seed. Null for the ~26% of complaints that don't match a crosswalk entry.

category.name: category
category.description: Company type from crosswalk: bank, credit_bureau, credit_union, debt_collector, mortgage_servicer, student_loan_servicer, fintech, or other. Null for uncrosswalked complaints.

is_crosswalked.name: is_crosswalked
is_crosswalked.description: TRUE if this complaint's company_name_normalized matched a crosswalk entry. ~74% of complaints are crosswalked.

zip_code_is_valid.name: zip_code_is_valid
zip_code_is_valid.description: TRUE if zip_code matches r'^\d{5}$' after staging cleanup. FALSE for null zips, non-numeric values, and the 48 known corrupted zips. Note: CFPB intentional 3-digit privacy zips (low-ZCTA-population + narrative consent) incorrectly pass as TRUE after staging LPAD — see docs/data-quality-notes.md § ZIP Code.

tags_is_servicemember.name: tags_is_servicemember
tags_is_servicemember.description: TRUE if the complaint's tags field includes 'Servicemember'. Covers active duty, National Guard, Reservists, Veterans, retirees, and their spouses/dependents. FALSE for pre-Feb 2016 complaints (structurally null — CFPB added tags in Release 11) and for complaints with no servicemember tag. Gate analysis on date_received >= '2016-02-01'.

tags_is_older_american.name: tags_is_older_american
tags_is_older_american.description: TRUE if the complaint's tags field includes 'Older American' (age ≥ 62 as reported by the submitter). FALSE for pre-Feb 2016 and untagged complaints. Same coverage floor as tags_is_servicemember.

is_dispute_era.name: is_dispute_era
is_dispute_era.description: TRUE for complaints received before 2017-04-24, the exact date CFPB discontinued the consumer_disputed field (Release 13). Gate any dispute-rate metric on this flag to avoid comparing full-coverage years against the post-discontinuation zero-fill period.

is_narrative_era.name: is_narrative_era
is_narrative_era.description: TRUE for complaints received on or after 2015-06-01, when CFPB launched the opt-in narrative feature. has_narrative is structurally FALSE before this date. Use as the floor for any narrative-rate metric.

days_to_company.name: days_to_company
days_to_company.description: Calendar days from date_received to date_sent_to_company, clamped to 0 via GREATEST. Measures CFPB routing speed, not company resolution time. 7,036 pre-2015 rows had a -1 day artifact (intake system clock issue); GREATEST clamps these to 0. Zero dominates 2015+ (75–92%) — reflects CFPB progressive automation of same-day forwarding, not a data artifact.

model.cfpb_complaints.fct_complaints.raw_code:
with complaints as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_complaints_with_company') }}
)

select
    complaint_id,
    date_received,
    date_sent_to_company,

    product,
    subproduct,
    product_normalized,
    case product_normalized
        when 'Mortgage'                                                                          then 'mortgage'
        when 'Credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports'      then 'credit_reporting'
        when 'Debt collection'                                                                   then 'debt_collection'
        when 'Checking or savings account'                                                       then 'banking'
        when 'Credit card or prepaid card'                                                       then 'card'
        when 'Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service'                               then 'payments'
        else 'other'
    end                                                                                          as product_category,
    subproduct_normalized,
    issue,
    subissue,

    farm_fingerprint(canonical_name)                                   as company_sk,
    company_name,
    company_name_normalized,
    canonical_name,
    category,
    is_crosswalked,
    state,
    zip_code,
    zip_code_is_valid,
    submitted_via,
    tags_is_servicemember,
    tags_is_older_american,
    consumer_consent_provided,

    company_response_to_consumer,
    company_public_response,
    timely_response,
    consumer_disputed,
    has_narrative,
    has_full_year_data,

    date_received < '2017-04-24'                                      as is_dispute_era,
    date_received >= '2015-06-01'                                     as is_narrative_era,
    greatest(date_diff(date_sent_to_company, date_received, day), 0) as days_to_company

from complaints

model.cfpb_complaints.int_complaints_wit.name: int_complaints_with_company
model.cfpb_complaints.int_complaints_wit.description: stg_cfpb_complaints left-joined to the company crosswalk on company_name_normalized. Adds canonical_name, category, and is_crosswalked to every complaint row. Grain: one row per complaint — the left join preserves all staging rows and raw_company_name is unique in stg_company_crosswalk, so no fan-out is possible. Reused by fct_complaints and any future company-level mart.

complaint_id.name: complaint_id
complaint_id.description: Natural primary key — carried from stg_cfpb_complaints.

canonical_name.name: canonical_name
canonical_name.description: Human-readable company name from the crosswalk (e.g. "JPMorgan Chase"). Null for complaints whose company_name_normalized has no crosswalk entry (~23% of volume by design — long-tail companies are uncategorized).

category.name: category
category.description: Company type from the crosswalk: bank, credit_bureau, debt_collector, mortgage_servicer, student_loan_servicer, fintech, or other. Null for uncrosswalked complaints.

company_parent_as_of.name: company_parent_as_of
company_parent_as_of.description: Effective date of entity change for rebranded companies (SunTrust, BB&T, Alliance Data). Null for current or historically-stable entities.

is_crosswalked.name: is_crosswalked
is_crosswalked.description: TRUE if the complaint's company_name_normalized matched a crosswalk entry. FALSE for long-tail companies not in the seed. Crosswalk covers ~77% of complaint volume (top 3 credit bureaus + top 30 others).

model.cfpb_complaints.int_complaints_wit.raw_code:
-- Left join CFPB complaints to the company crosswalk on the normalized company name.
-- Adds canonical_name, category, and is_crosswalked to every complaint row.
-- Grain: one row per complaint (left join preserves all staging rows; no fan-out
-- because raw_company_name is unique in stg_company_crosswalk).

with complaints as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_cfpb_complaints') }}
),

crosswalk as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_company_crosswalk') }}
)

select
    c.*,
    xw.canonical_name,
    xw.category,
    xw.parent_as_of                         as company_parent_as_of,
    xw.raw_company_name is not null         as is_crosswalked

from complaints c
left join crosswalk xw
    on c.company_name_normalized = xw.raw_company_name

    xw.raw_company_name is not null         as is_crosswalked

from complaints c
left join crosswalk xw
    on c.company_name_normalized = xw.raw_company_name

model.cfpb_complaints.stg_cfpb_complaint.name: stg_cfpb_complaints
model.cfpb_complaints.stg_cfpb_complaint.description: Staging pass over `raw.cfpb_complaints`. String columns trimmed; state uppercased. All source rows included; use `has_full_year_data = true` to restrict to complete calendar years (2012–2022). One row per complaint.

date_sent_to_company.name: date_sent_to_company
date_sent_to_company.description: Date CFPB forwarded the complaint to the named company. 7,036 rows (~0.2%) have date_sent_to_company < date_received by exactly 1 day, all between 2012-01-22 and 2014-04-26. Systematic intake-system artifact (timezone/batch-processing clock issue); rows are kept and assert_complaint_dates_ordered runs at severity: warn.

product.name: product
product.description: Top-level product taxonomy (18 distinct values). accepted_values at severity: warn — values list written from general CFPB taxonomy knowledge, not verified against actual data. Promote to severity: error after running SELECT DISTINCT product.

subproduct.name: subproduct
subproduct.description: Product subcategory; ~7% null. Raw value from source — preserved for auditability.

product_normalized.name: product_normalized
product_normalized.description: product with legacy CFPB taxonomy merged into current labels. Eight old categories consolidated: Bank account or service → Checking or savings account; Consumer Loan → Vehicle loan or lease or Payday loan, title loan, or personal loan (by subproduct); Credit card → Credit card or prepaid card; Credit reporting → Credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports; Money transfers → Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service; Payday loan → Payday loan, title loan, or personal loan; Prepaid card → Credit card or prepaid card; Virtual currency → Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service. Unrecognized values pass through unchanged. not_null at severity: error — product is never null and the CASE has an ELSE branch, so a null here means broken logic.

subproduct_normalized.name: subproduct_normalized
subproduct_normalized.description: subproduct with legacy labels merged into current equivalents, consistent with product_normalized. Key changes: Bank account or service subproduct label differences resolved; Consumer Loan vehicle subproducts renamed to Loan / Lease; legacy single-product null subproducts filled (Credit card → General-purpose credit card or charge card, Credit reporting → Credit reporting, Payday loan → Payday loan); Prepaid card subproducts mapped to Credit card or prepaid card equivalents; Debt collection old-style subproducts normalized (added "debt" suffix: Credit card → Credit card debt, Medical → Medical debt, etc.; Non-federal student loan → Private student loan debt). Unrecognized values pass through unchanged.

company_name.name: company_name
company_name.description: Free-text company name as submitted. 6,694 distinct values; parent corporations mixed with subsidiaries. Canonicalization happens downstream via crosswalk seed.

company_name_normalized.name: company_name_normalized
company_name_normalized.description: Derived from company_name: uppercased, internal whitespace collapsed to a single space, trailing period/comma/semicolon stripped. Used for grouping and crosswalk matching without altering the raw value.

state.name: state
state.description: 2-char US state/territory/military code, uppercased. Null remapped to 'not-provided'. "UNITED STATES MINOR OUTLYING ISLANDS" (305 rows) remapped to ISO code 'UM'. Any other long-form value remapped to 'not-provided' as a safety catch. Includes territories (PR, VI, GU, AS, MP, UM), military APO codes (AA, AE, AP), and freely associated states (FM, MH, PW). accepted_values at severity: error — values list confirmed against actual data (63 distinct raw values, all accounted for in the CASE expression or safety catch).

zip_code.name: zip_code
zip_code.description: ZIP code as published by CFPB. Most complaints have a standard 5-digit ZIP. Exception: complaints where the consumer consented to narrative publication AND lives in a ZCTA with fewer than 20,000 people receive a 3-digit ZIP (CFPB privacy rule; Release 10, June 2015). Staging incorrectly LPADs these 3-digit values to 5-digit (e.g., raw "17.0" → "00017" instead of "017") — they are numeric and pass zip_code_is_valid, so the flag does NOT catch them. See docs/data-quality-notes.md § ZIP Code for impact and fix approach. No XXXXX masked values observed in this dataset.

tags.name: tags
tags.description: Special-population tag. 89% null. Three distinct non-null values: "Servicemember", "Older American", "Older American, Servicemember" (always Older American–first). Parse with LIKE '%Servicemember%' and LIKE '%Older American%' to handle the combined value correctly. CFPB definitions: Older American = submitter reports consumer age ≥ 62. Servicemember = active duty, National Guard, Reservist, Veteran, or retiree — AND spouses/dependents of servicemembers. Coverage: field added February 2016 (CFPB Release 11). Pre-2016 complaints (2012–2015 in our dataset) are structurally null — not a data quality issue. Use 2016 as the effective floor for any tags-based analysis.

tags_is_servicemember.name: tags_is_servicemember
tags_is_servicemember.description: TRUE if tags includes 'Servicemember'. Covers active duty, National Guard, Reservists, Veterans, retirees, and their spouses/dependents. FALSE for pre-Feb 2016 complaints (structurally null) and untagged complaints. Gate any analysis on date_received >= '2016-02-01'.

tags_is_older_american.name: tags_is_older_american
tags_is_older_american.description: TRUE if tags includes 'Older American' (age ≥ 62 as reported by submitter). FALSE for pre-Feb 2016 and untagged complaints. Same coverage floor as tags_is_servicemember.

zip_code_is_valid.name: zip_code_is_valid
zip_code_is_valid.description: TRUE if zip_code matches r'^\d{5}$' after staging cleanup. FALSE for null zips, non-numeric values, and 48 known corrupted zips. Note: CFPB intentional 3-digit privacy zips incorrectly pass as TRUE after staging LPAD — see docs/data-quality-notes.md § ZIP Code.

submitted_via.name: submitted_via
submitted_via.description: Intake channel (Web, Phone, Referral, etc.). accepted_values at severity: warn — values list unverified. Promote to severity: error after running SELECT DISTINCT submitted_via.

company_response_to_consumer.name: company_response_to_consumer
company_response_to_consumer.description: Resolution category returned by the company. 4 null rows mapped to 'Untimely response' (assumption: no response filed = untimely). not_null at severity: error — nulls coalesced away in staging.

company_public_response.name: company_public_response
company_public_response.description: Optional public statement from the company. Null (56%) mapped to 'not-provided' — sentinel chosen over NULL so GROUP BY includes a visible bucket without special COALESCE handling downstream. The verbose label "Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response" (1.3M rows) is collapsed to "Company chooses not to provide a public response" — the consumer response detail belongs in company_response_to_consumer, not here. not_null at severity: error — nulls mapped to 'not-provided' in staging.

consumer_consent_provided.name: consumer_consent_provided
consumer_consent_provided.description: Whether the consumer consented to narrative publication. Null (25%) mapped to 'not-provided' — same sentinel pattern as company_public_response; keeps GROUP BY clean without downstream COALESCE. Values after staging: Consent provided, Consent not provided, Consent withdrawn, Other, not-provided. not_null at severity: error — nulls coalesced away in staging. Population timing: "Consent provided" and "Consent not provided" populate 60 days after the complaint is sent to the company OR after the company files an optional public response, whichever comes first — explains why recent complaints have more nulls (window not yet elapsed at extract time). "N/A" populates immediately for pre-March 2015 complaints or cases where the narrative option was unavailable. "Other" means the complaint doesn't meet criteria for narrative publication.

timely_response.name: timely_response
timely_response.description: Whether the company responded within CFPB's SLA.

consumer_disputed.name: consumer_disputed
consumer_disputed.description: Legacy field. CFPB stopped collecting mid-2017: 100% fill through 2016, 29.8% in 2017, 0% from 2018 onward — 78% null overall. Gate any dispute-rate metric on date_received < '2017-01-01' to avoid comparing full-coverage years against empty ones.

has_narrative.name: has_narrative
has_narrative.description: Derived at raw materialization: TRUE if the source row had a non-empty consumer_complaint_narrative (text dropped to reduce table size 2.31 GB → 768 MB). Zero before June 2015 — CFPB launched the opt-in narrative feature then. The 2015 rate (32.5%) reflects a partial year; treat 2016 as the effective floor for narrative analysis. not_null at severity: error — derived boolean, always TRUE or FALSE.

has_full_year_data.name: has_full_year_data
has_full_year_data.description: TRUE when date_received falls in a complete calendar year (2012–2022). FALSE for 2011 (December stub, 2,536 rows) and 2023 (frozen at 2023-03-23, ~244K rows). Filter on this flag for clean year-over-year trend analysis; leave unfiltered when the question involves partial-year data (e.g., Q1 2023).

model.cfpb_complaints.stg_cfpb_complaint.raw_code:
-- Thin staging pass over raw.cfpb_complaints.
-- Renames: none needed (source is already snake_case).
-- Transforms: trim all string columns; upper-case 2-char state code.
-- company_name_normalized: UPPER + collapsed whitespace + trailing punct stripped.
-- product_normalized / subproduct_normalized: seed joins to product_mapping and subproduct_mapping.
--   Consumer Loan + vehicle subproduct is the only case where product_normalized depends on
--   subproduct; handled inline. All other product renames come from the product_mapping seed.
-- has_full_year_data: false for 2011 (stub, Dec only) and 2023 (partial, frozen 2023-03-23).
--   Filter on this flag for clean year-over-year trend analysis.

with source as (
    select * from {{ source('raw', 'cfpb_complaints') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        complaint_id,
        date_received,
        date_sent_to_company,

        trim(product)                        as product,
        trim(subproduct)                     as subproduct,
        trim(issue)                          as issue,
        trim(subissue)                       as subissue,

        trim(company_name)                   as company_name,
        regexp_replace(
            regexp_replace(upper(trim(company_name)), r'\s+', ' '),
            r'[.,;]+$', ''
        )                                    as company_name_normalized,
        case
            when state is null                                                    then 'not-provided'
            when upper(trim(state)) = 'UNITED STATES MINOR OUTLYING ISLANDS'     then 'UM'
            when length(trim(state)) > 2                                          then 'not-provided'
            else upper(trim(state))
        end                                  as state,
        -- Raw zip_code is float-formatted ('30349.0') — BigQuery CSV import artifact.
        -- Also strip trailing '-': artifact of users beginning to type zip+4 (e.g. '3008-').
        -- SAFE_CAST: non-numeric values pass through via COALESCE so zip_code_is_valid
        -- can flag them; NULL source stays NULL.
        coalesce(
            lpad(
                cast(safe_cast(
                    regexp_replace(
                        regexp_replace(trim(zip_code), r'\.0$', ''),
                        r'-$', ''
                    )
                as int64) as string),
                5, '0'
            ),
            trim(zip_code)
        )                                    as zip_code,

        trim(tags)                                              as tags,
        -- Tags added Feb 2016; pre-2016 complaints are structurally null — not missing data.
        coalesce(trim(tags) like '%Servicemember%', false)     as tags_is_servicemember,
        coalesce(trim(tags) like '%Older American%', false)    as tags_is_older_american,

        trim(submitted_via)                  as submitted_via,

        -- 4 nulls exist; assumption: no response filed = untimely response.
        coalesce(trim(company_response_to_consumer), 'Untimely response')
                                             as company_response_to_consumer,

        -- Verbose label ("...and chooses not to provide a public response") is the post-2015
        -- CFPB form; it restates company_response_to_consumer info that belongs in the other
        -- field. Collapse to the shorter label; null = no public statement filed.
        case
            when trim(company_public_response) = 'Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response'
                then 'Company chooses not to provide a public response'
            when company_public_response is null
                then 'not-provided'
            else trim(company_public_response)
        end                                  as company_public_response,

        coalesce(trim(consumer_consent_provided), 'not-provided')
                                             as consumer_consent_provided,

        timely_response,
        consumer_disputed,
        has_narrative,

        extract(year from date_received) between 2012 and 2022
                                             as has_full_year_data

    from source
),

normalized as (
    select
        r.*,
        coalesce(
            -- Consumer Loan + vehicle subproduct: product_normalized depends on subproduct
            case
                when r.product = 'Consumer Loan' and r.subproduct in ('Vehicle loan', 'Vehicle lease')
                    then 'Vehicle loan or lease'
            end,
            pm.product_normalized,
            r.product
        )                                    as product_normalized,
        coalesce(sm.subproduct_normalized, r.subproduct)
                                             as subproduct_normalized,
        coalesce(regexp_contains(r.zip_code, r'^\d{5}$'), false)
                                             as zip_code_is_valid
    from renamed r
    left join {{ ref('product_mapping') }} pm
        on r.product = pm.raw_product
    left join {{ ref('subproduct_mapping') }} sm
        on r.product = sm.raw_product
        and coalesce(r.subproduct, '') = coalesce(sm.raw_subproduct, '')
)

select * from normalized

test.cfpb_complaints.assert_dispute_era_.name: assert_dispute_era_nulls
test.cfpb_complaints.assert_dispute_era_.raw_code:
-- consumer_disputed has 100% fill through 2016, drops to 0% by 2018. CFPB
-- discontinued the field on 2017-04-24 (Release 13). Every complaint outside
-- the dispute era (is_dispute_era = false) should have a NULL consumer_disputed.
-- Failures here mean either the era cutoff date is wrong or unexpected non-null
-- values crept into the post-discontinuation period.
{{ config(severity='warn') }}

source.cfpb_complaints.raw.cfpb_complain.name: cfpb_complaints
source.cfpb_complaints.raw.cfpb_complain.source_description: Raw tables loaded outside dbt. CFPB materialized from the BigQuery public dataset `bigquery-public-data.cfpb_complaints.complaint_database` (frozen 2023-03-25, narrative column dropped). FDIC loaded from the FDIC BankFind active-institutions export.
source.cfpb_complaints.raw.cfpb_complain.description: CFPB Consumer Complaint Database, 2011-12 through 2023-03. 3,458,906 rows. Narrative text dropped at materialize time (2.31 GB → 768 MB); replaced with boolean `has_narrative`.

product.name: product
product.description: Top-level product taxonomy. 18 distinct values, 0% null.

issue.name: issue
issue.description: Top-level issue taxonomy. 165 distinct values, 0% null.

company_name.name: company_name
company_name.description: Free-text company name as submitted. 6,694 distinct values. Mixes parent corporations and subsidiaries — normalization target downstream.

state.name: state
state.description: 2-char US state/territory code. 63 distinct values, 1.2% null.

tags.name: tags
tags.description: Special-population tag (Older American, Servicemember, or both). 3 distinct values, 88.9% null.

company_public_response.name: company_public_response
company_public_response.description: Optional public statement from the company. 11 distinct values, 55.6% null.

consumer_disputed.name: consumer_disputed
consumer_disputed.description: Legacy field — CFPB stopped collecting ~2017. 77.8% null.

has_narrative.name: has_narrative
has_narrative.description: Derived at raw materialize time. TRUE if the source row had a non-empty `consumer_complaint_narrative`; the text itself was dropped.

source.cfpb_complaints.raw.fdic_active_b.name: fdic_active_banks_lean
source.cfpb_complaints.raw.fdic_active_b.source_description: Raw tables loaded outside dbt. CFPB materialized from the BigQuery public dataset `bigquery-public-data.cfpb_complaints.complaint_database` (frozen 2023-03-25, narrative column dropped). FDIC loaded from the FDIC BankFind active-institutions export.
source.cfpb_complaints.raw.fdic_active_b.description: Active FDIC-insured institutions, trimmed from 117 to 95 columns. All rows have `active = TRUE`. 4,756 rows.

fdic_certificate_number.name: fdic_certificate_number
fdic_certificate_number.description: FDIC certificate number — natural primary key.

institution_name.name: institution_name
institution_name.description: Operating bank legal name (e.g. "JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association").

top_holder.name: top_holder
top_holder.description: Parent holding company name (e.g. "JPMORGAN CHASE & CO."). Primary bridge to CFPB `company_name` for bank-side complaint joins.

cfpb_supervisory_flag.name: cfpb_supervisory_flag
cfpb_supervisory_flag.description: Whether the institution is under CFPB supervision.

total_assets.name: total_assets
total_assets.description: Total assets in USD. Primary sort key for size-based analysis.

ots_region.name: ots_region
ots_region.description: OTS region. Known "West" vs "Western" inconsistency in source — normalize in staging.

row_pretax_quarterly.name: row_pretax_quarterly
row_pretax_quarterly.description: Pre-tax quarterly return on equity. Column name is a source typo (row_ should be roe_).

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        tables.project_id as table_catalog,
        tables.dataset_id as table_schema,
        coalesce(REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^(.+)[0-9]{8}$'), tables.table_id) as table_name,
        tables.table_id as shard_name,
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^.+([0-9]{8})$') as shard_index,
        REGEXP_CONTAINS(tables.table_id, '^.+[0-9]{8}$') and tables.type = 1 as is_date_shard,
        case
            when materialized_views.table_name is not null then 'materialized view'
            when tables.type = 1 then 'table'
            when tables.type = 2 then 'view'
            else 'external'
        end as table_type,
        tables.type = 1 as is_table,
        JSON_VALUE(table_description.option_value) as table_comment,
        tables.size_bytes,
        tables.row_count
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='__TABLES__') }} tables
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='MATERIALIZED_VIEWS') }} materialized_views
        on materialized_views.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and materialized_views.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and materialized_views.table_name = tables.table_id
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='TABLE_OPTIONS') }} table_description
        on table_description.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and table_description.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and table_description.table_name = tables.table_id
        and table_description.option_name = 'description'
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.name: _bigquery__get_tables_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_tables_sql() %}
    select distinct
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        is_date_shard,
        table_type,
        is_table,
        table_comment
    from table_shards
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        max(shard_name) as latest_shard_name,
        min(shard_index) as shard_min,
        max(shard_index) as shard_max,
        count(shard_index) as shard_count,
        sum(size_bytes) as size_bytes,
        sum(row_count) as row_count
    from table_shards
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_columns_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_columns_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        columns.table_catalog,
        columns.table_schema,
        columns.table_name as shard_name,
        coalesce(paths.field_path, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        -- invent a row number to account for nested fields
        -- BQ does not treat these nested properties as independent fields
        row_number() over (
            partition by
                columns.table_catalog,
                columns.table_schema,
                columns.table_name
            order by
                columns.ordinal_position,
                paths.field_path
        ) as column_index,
        coalesce(paths.data_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        paths.description as column_comment,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then 1 else 0 end as is_partitioning_column,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then paths.field_path end as partition_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then 1 else 0 end as is_clustering_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then paths.field_path end as cluster_column,
        columns.clustering_ordinal_position
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMNS') }} columns
    join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS') }} paths
        on paths.table_catalog = columns.table_catalog
        and paths.table_schema = columns.table_schema
        and paths.table_name = columns.table_name
        and paths.column_name = columns.column_name
    where columns.ordinal_position is not null
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        shard_name,
        max(is_partitioning_column) = 1 as is_partitioned,
        max(partition_column) as partition_column,
        max(is_clustering_column) = 1 as is_clustered,
        array_to_string(
            array_agg(
                cluster_column ignore nulls
                order by clustering_ordinal_position
            ), ', '
        ) as clustering_columns
    from columns
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.name: _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql() %}
    select
        tables.table_catalog as table_database,
        tables.table_schema,
        case
            when tables.is_date_shard then concat(tables.table_name, '*')
            else tables.table_name
        end as table_name,
        tables.table_type,
        tables.table_comment,
        -- coalesce column metadata fields to ensure they are non-null for catalog generation
        -- external table columns are not present in COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS
        coalesce(columns.column_name, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        coalesce(columns.column_index, 1) as column_index,
        coalesce(columns.column_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        coalesce(columns.column_comment, '') as column_comment,

        'Shard count' as `stats__date_shards__label`,
        table_stats.shard_count as `stats__date_shards__value`,
        'The number of date shards in this table' as `stats__date_shards__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shards__include`,

        'Shard (min)' as `stats__date_shard_min__label`,
        table_stats.shard_min as `stats__date_shard_min__value`,
        'The first date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_min__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_min__include`,

        'Shard (max)' as `stats__date_shard_max__label`,
        table_stats.shard_max as `stats__date_shard_max__value`,
        'The last date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_max__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_max__include`,

        '# Rows' as `stats__num_rows__label`,
        table_stats.row_count as `stats__num_rows__value`,
        'Approximate count of rows in this table' as `stats__num_rows__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_rows__include`,

        'Approximate Size' as `stats__num_bytes__label`,
        table_stats.size_bytes as `stats__num_bytes__value`,
        'Approximate size of table as reported by BigQuery' as `stats__num_bytes__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_bytes__include`,

        'Partitioned By' as `stats__partitioning_type__label`,
        column_stats.partition_column as `stats__partitioning_type__value`,
        'The partitioning column for this table' as `stats__partitioning_type__description`,
        column_stats.is_partitioned as `stats__partitioning_type__include`,

        'Clustered By' as `stats__clustering_fields__label`,
        column_stats.clustering_columns as `stats__clustering_fields__value`,
        'The clustering columns for this table' as `stats__clustering_fields__description`,
        column_stats.is_clustered as `stats__clustering_fields__include`

    from tables
    join table_stats
        on table_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and table_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and table_stats.table_name = tables.table_name
    left join column_stats
        on column_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and column_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and column_stats.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
    left join columns
        on columns.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and columns.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and columns.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
{% endmacro %}

doc.dbt.__overview__.name: __overview__
doc.dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--select` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- [What is dbt](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/introduction)?
- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [dbt Community](https://www.getdbt.com/community/) for questions and discussion

exposure.cfpb_complaints.cfpb_complaints.name: cfpb_complaints_dashboard
exposure.cfpb_complaints.cfpb_complaints.description: Data Studio dashboard with two live pages: (1) complaint volume over time by product_category (year-quarter granularity, credit_reporting excluded to avoid scale distortion), (2) top 20 companies by complaint volume colored by category. A third page (timely response rate by product_category) is planned.

metric.cfpb_complaints.avg_days_to_compa.name: avg_days_to_company
metric.cfpb_complaints.avg_days_to_compa.description: Average calendar days from complaint receipt to CFPB forwarding to the company. Measures CFPB routing speed, not company resolution time. Zero dominates 2015+ (75–92%) as CFPB progressively automated same-day forwarding — low values reflect operational efficiency, not missing data.
metric.cfpb_complaints.avg_days_to_compa.label: Avg Days to Company

metric.cfpb_complaints.dispute_rate.name: dispute_rate
metric.cfpb_complaints.dispute_rate.description: Share of complaints where the consumer disputed the company's response. Filtered to is_dispute_era = true (pre-2017-04-24): CFPB discontinued the consumer_disputed field on that exact date (Release 13), so computing this metric across all years produces a meaningless denominator.
metric.cfpb_complaints.dispute_rate.label: Dispute Rate

metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_rate.name: narrative_rate
metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_rate.description: Share of complaints with a consumer narrative attached (opt-in). Filtered to is_narrative_era = true (on or after 2015-06-01): CFPB launched the narrative feature in that release; pre-era values are structurally false and would suppress the rate toward zero.
metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_rate.label: Narrative Rate

metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_r.name: timely_response_rate
metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_r.description: Share of complaints where the company responded within the CFPB deadline. Universally high (>95% for top institutions) — most useful as a cross-company comparator, not an absolute signal.
metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_r.label: Timely Response Rate

metric.cfpb_complaints.complaint_count.name: complaint_count
metric.cfpb_complaints.complaint_count.description: Count of all complaints in the dataset (2012–2022).
metric.cfpb_complaints.complaint_count.label: Total Complaints

metric.cfpb_complaints.disputed_count.name: disputed_count
metric.cfpb_complaints.disputed_count.description: Count of complaints where the consumer disputed the response. Only meaningful when filtered to is_dispute_era = true.
metric.cfpb_complaints.disputed_count.label: Disputed Count

metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_count.name: narrative_count
metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_count.description: Count of complaints with an attached consumer narrative. Only meaningful when filtered to is_narrative_era = true.
metric.cfpb_complaints.narrative_count.label: Narrative Count

metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_c.name: timely_response_count
metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_c.description: Count of complaints with a timely company response.
metric.cfpb_complaints.timely_response_c.label: Timely Response Count

semantic_model.cfpb_complaints.complaint.name: complaints
semantic_model.cfpb_complaints.complaint.description: Fact table grain: one row per CFPB complaint (2012–2022). Primary time dimension is date_received. company entity joins to dim_company via company_sk.

measures.name: timely_response_count
measures.description: Count of complaints with a timely company response.
measures.expr: case when timely_response then 1 else 0 end

measures.name: disputed_count
measures.description: Count of complaints where the consumer disputed the response. Only meaningful when filtered to is_dispute_era = true.
measures.expr: case when consumer_disputed = 'Yes' then 1 else 0 end

measures.name: narrative_count
measures.description: Count of complaints with an attached consumer narrative. Only meaningful when filtered to is_narrative_era = true.
measures.expr: case when has_narrative then 1 else 0 end

measures.name: avg_days_to_company
measures.description: Average CFPB routing time from receipt to forwarding. Clamped to 0 — the 7,036 pre-2015 -1-day intake artifacts are zeroed out.

dimensions.name: is_dispute_era
dimensions.description: TRUE for complaints before 2017-04-24. Gate dispute_rate on this dimension — post-era consumer_disputed is structurally null.

dimensions.name: is_narrative_era
dimensions.description: TRUE for complaints on or after 2015-06-01. Gate narrative_rate on this dimension — pre-era has_narrative is structurally false.
